chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-2212 of 2023 Date Order with Signature of Judge For hearing of main case 14-03-2025 Mr. Mehmood A. H. Baloch Advocate for the Petitioner Mr. Danish Warsi Advocate holds brief for Mr. Muhammad Nishat Warsi Advocate for the Respondent PQA Mr. Kashif Nazir, Assistant Attorney General Four petitioners have filed this petition in 2023, claiming to have ancestral title to the land that presently falls within the ambit of Port Qasim Authority. On such basis the Petitioners claim entitlement for employment, hence this petition. The order sheet denotes that on the very first day learned counsel was put on notice to place on record documents, the terms of which were alleged to have been violated in respect of any recruitment including that initiated in June 2022. Admittedly, no such documents are on file. The right and entitlement to immovable property claimed by the Petitioners is not substantiated from the record and even otherwise, direct recourse to writ jurisdiction is unwarranted for assertion thereof. The Respondent, Port Qasim Authority, is a public body and employment thereof ought to be predicted on merit and no case is made out to entertain any claim in contradiction thereof. The petition is found to be prima facie misconceived, frivolous and even otherwise devoid of merit, hence, dismissed with cost of Rs.5,000/- each to be paid to the High Court Clinic. Judge Judge Amjad PS
